title: Mickelia bernoullii
taxonomy: Mickelia bernoullii is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Mickelia, which is a group of vascular plants. Mickelia bernoullii is a type of fern, specifically a member of the order Polypodiales. This species is native to tropical regions and is often found in humid environments. Mickelia bernoullii is also known by its synonym, Grammatopteris bernoullii.

intro: Mickelia bernoullii, also known as Philodendron bernoullii, is a species of flowering plant in the family Arumaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative properties. The bioactive constituents of Mickelia bernoullii include philodendrine, philodendron alkaloids, and other steroidal compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Mickelia bernoullii is commonly prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create sleep-promoting and anxiolytic remedies.
